scenarios.name: Buncombe-Preserving Tornado
scenarios.description: The canonical demo scenario. Designed so every number matches the Buncombe scene in the white paper.
scenarios.trigger_directive: [SYSTEM EVENT] The NWS has just issued a tornado warning. The active warning layer has been updated with the polygon. Produce the proactive situational briefing for this event following your disaster playbook. Under 120 words. Use tool calls for every number.
scenarios.narrative_notes: These numbers are baked into the synthetic data. The generate_cascade_data.py script places mobile home parks, schools, and facilities specifically to produce these exact counts when the warning polygon is applied. Every number matches the Buncombe scene in the white paper.

scenarios.name: Shasta County · Redding Fire Perimeter
scenarios.description: Active wildfire in western Shasta County, California — fire footprint northwest of Redding near the Whiskeytown / Buckhorn corridor. Every figure Claude reports is pulled live from the DEMO_FireArea_* layers intersected with the fire perimeter.
scenarios.trigger_directive: [SYSTEM EVENT] An evacuation warning has just been issued for an active wildfire in western Shasta County, California, northwest of Redding. The active warning layer now shows the current fire perimeter. Produce the proactive situational briefing for this event following your wildfire playbook. Under 120 words. Use tool calls for every number — population inside the perimeter (DEMO_FireArea_Census_Tracts), fire stations (DEMO_FireArea_Fire_Stations), police stations (DEMO_FireArea_Police_Stations), hospitals (DEMO_FireArea_Hospitals), dialysis clinics (DEMO_FireArea_Dialysis_Clinics), and Red Cross shelters (DEMO_FireArea_Red_Cross_Shelters).
scenarios.narrative_notes: Numbers are not baked. The perimeter sits in western Shasta County CA, entirely separate from the Cascade County tornado scenario. Fire-scenario layers (DEMO_FireArea_*) have real facility names — Mercy Medical Center Redding, Shasta Regional, DaVita dialysis, etc. — to support a concrete briefing.
